/**
 * @component Alert
 * @description Displays a dismissible message with various types (default, success, error, info, warning), an optional title, body text, action button, and close button.
 * 
 * @type {{
 * 	 isOpen: boolean,              // Controls visibility
 * 	 onclose: () => void,          // Callback when closed
 *   type?: 'default' | 'success' | 'error' | 'info' | 'warning'; // The type of alert, determining its appearance and icon. Defaults to 'default'.
 *   title: string; // The main title text for the alert.
 *   body?: string; // Optional body text providing more details.
 *   buttonText?: string; // Optional text for an action button. If provided, the button is displayed.
 *   showCloseButton?: boolean; // Whether to display the close (x) button. Defaults to true.
 *   class?: string; // Optional CSS classes to apply to the root alert element.
 *   position?: 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'lower-left' | 'lower-right' | 'center-bottom'; // Optional fixed positioning on the screen.
 * 	 autoCloseSeconds?: number; // Optional. If provided and positive, alert will close automatically after this many seconds.
 * }}
 * 
 * @example
 * 
 * // ParentComponent.svelte
 *   import Alert from './Alert.svelte'; // Path to Alert.svelte
 *   let isAlertVisible = $state(true);
 * 
 *   function handleAlertInteraction() {
 *     // This function is called when the alert's close button
 *     // OR its action button (if present) is clicked,
 *     // as both currently trigger the 'onclose' callback.
 *     isAlertVisible = false;
 *     console.log('Alert closed or action button clicked.');
 *   }
 * 
 * {#if isAlertVisible}
 *   <Alert
 *     isOpen={isAlertVisible}
 *     onclose={handleAlertInteraction}
 *     type="error"
 *     title="Save Failed"
 *     body="Your changes could not be saved. Please try again."
 *     buttonText="Retry Save"
 *     position="top-right"
 *     showCloseButton={true}
 *   />
 * {/if}
 * 
 * <!-- To show the alert again, you would typically set isAlertVisible = true; -->
 * <!-- <button onclick={() => isAlertVisible = true}>Show Alert Again</button> -->
 */

/**
 * @component AnimatedStepper
 * @description A reusable stepper component that handles multi-step flows with smooth animations. It dynamically renders components with slide transitions between steps.
 * 
 * @type {{
 *   components: Record<string, any>; // A map of component names to Svelte component constructors.
 *   steps: Array<{
 *     componentName: string; // The key from the 'components' object to render for this step.
 *     params?: Record<string, any>; // Props to pass to the step component.
 *     next?: string | ((detail: any) => Promise<string | null> | string | null); // The name of the next step, or a function that returns it.
 *     back?: string; // Optional override for the back button target.
 *     canGoBack?: boolean; // If false, the back button is disabled for this step.
 *     contentClasses?: string; // Custom CSS classes for the content wrapper of this step. Defaults to 'bg-white py-4 px-6 rounded-t-lg'.
 *     contentAlign?: 'top' | 'center' | 'bottom' | 'end'; // Overrides the stepper's contentAlign for this specific step.
 *     buttonSize?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'; // Overrides the back button size for this step.
 *     backButtonClass?: string; // Custom CSS classes for the back button for this step.
 *   }>; // An array of step configuration objects.
 *   onComplete?: (stepData: Record<string, any>) => void; // Callback fired when the final step is completed.
 *   onStepChange?: (detail: { step: number; direction: 'forward' | 'back'; stepData: Record<string, any> }) => void; // Callback fired when the step changes.
 *   animationDuration?: number; // Duration of the slide animation in milliseconds.
 *   containerHeight?: string; // CSS height for the stepper container (e.g., '50vh', '300px').
 *   contentAlign?: 'top' | 'center' | 'bottom' | 'end'; // Vertical alignment of content within the container.
 *   stepHeader?: () => void; // Optional snippet rendered above the step component.
 *   initialStepName?: string | null; // Optional: The componentName of the step to start on.
 *   class?: string; // Optional CSS classes for the root component element.
 *   contentClasses?: string; // Default CSS classes for the content wrapper of each step.
 *   history?: any[]; // Bindable: Exposes the step history array.
 *   currentStepComponent?: object; // Bindable: Exposes the current step component configuration object.
 * }}
 * 
 * @description The stepper automatically passes `onNext`, `onback`, and `ongoto` callback props to the currently active step component to handle navigation.
 * 
 * @event onComplete - Fired when the final step is completed. The event detail is the accumulated `stepData` object.
 * @event onStepChange - Fired when the step changes. The event detail is an object: `{ step: number; direction: 'forward' | 'back'; stepData: Record<string, any> }`.
 * 
 * @function back - Programmatically triggers the back navigation.
 * 
 * @example
 * ```svelte
 *   import AnimatedStepper from './AnimatedStepper.svelte';
 *   import StepOne from './StepOne.svelte';
 *   import StepTwo from './StepTwo.svelte';
 * 
 *   const components = {
 *     'step-one': StepOne,
 *     'step-two': StepTwo
 *   };
 * 
 *   const steps = [
 *     {
 *       componentName: 'step-one',
 *       params: { title: 'Welcome' },
 *       next: 'step-two',
 *       canGoBack: false
 *     },
 *     {
 *       componentName: 'step-two',
 *       params: {},
 *       next: null, // Indicates this is the last step
 *       canGoBack: true
 *     }
 *   ];
 * 
 *   function handleComplete(finalData) {
 *     console.log('Stepper finished with data:', finalData);
 *   }
 * 
 * <AnimatedStepper {components} {steps} {onComplete} />
 * ```
 */

/**
 * @component Logo
 * @description Displays the Mamamia logo as an inline SVG. The `mm-logo-light.svg` content is embedded,
 * and its paths use `fill="currentColor"`.
 * The color can be controlled via CSS:
 * 1. By the `variant` prop ('light' for white, 'dark' for black using Tailwind text classes applied to the SVG).
 * 2. By the `customColor` prop (sets an inline style for `color` on the SVG).
 * 3. By passing Tailwind text color utility classes via the `class` prop to the SVG.
 * The `customColor` prop takes precedence, followed by text color utilities in `class`, then the `variant` prop.
 * @type {{
 *   width?: number | string,       // Optional: Explicit width for the SVG. If not set, width is controlled by CSS via the 'class' prop (e.g., w-auto, h-9).
 *   variant?: 'dark' | 'light',    // Default color variant: 'dark' (applies `text-black`), 'light' (applies `text-white`). Overridden by `customColor` or text color utilities in `class`.
 *   customColor?: string,          // Optional: A CSS color string (e.g., 'red', '#FF0000', 'var(--mm-colors-denim)') to set a custom logo color via inline style. Takes precedence.
 *   class?: string                 // Optional CSS classes for styling (e.g., size, margins, or overriding text color).
 * }}
 */

/**
 * @component LinkButton
 * @description A component that styles an anchor (`<a>`) tag to look like a button. Supports different variants, sizes, and states. It also forwards any additional attributes (like `onclick`) to the underlying `<a>` element.
 * 
 * @example
 * <LinkButton href="/some-page" variant="filled" size="md">
 *   Internal Link
 * </LinkButton>
 * 
 * @example
 * <!-- External link opening in a new tab -->
 * <LinkButton href="https://example.com" variant="outline" isExternal={true}>
 *   External Link
 * </LinkButton>
 * 
 * @example
 * <!-- Circular link button, often for an icon -->
 * <LinkButton href="/add" variant="filled" circle aria-label="Add item">
 *   <Icon name="plus" />
 * </LinkButton>
 * 
 * @example
 * <!-- Custom styling with CSS variables -->
 * <LinkButton
 *   href="/custom"
 *   style="--mm-button-background-color: var(--mm-colors-denim); --mm-button-color: white;"
 * >
 *   Custom Button
 * </LinkButton>
 * 
 * @type {{
 *   variant?: 'filled' | 'tonal' | 'outline' | 'ghost' | 'filled-destructive' | 'outline-destructive' | 'ghost-destructive' | 'subscribe'; // Button style variant
 *   size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg';                           // Button size
 *   href: string;                                       // The URL the link points to.
 *   isExternal?: boolean;                                // If true, adds target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer".
 *   circle?: boolean;                                    // Whether the button is circular.
 *   'aria-label'?: string;                               // Accessible label for screen readers.
 *   'aria-labelledby'?: string;                          // ID of element containing accessible label.
 *   class?: string;                                      // Additional CSS classes.
 *   allowPrefetch?: boolean;                             // Prevent prefetching causing page hangs, must opt in.
 *   darkMode?: boolean;                                  // Apply dark mode specific styles.
 *   style?: string;                                      // Inline styles for CSS variable overrides.
 *   onclick?: (event: MouseEvent) => void;               // Click event handler.
 *   ripple?: boolean;                                    // Whether to show a ripple effect on click.
 *   children?: import('svelte/elements').Snippet;         // Snippet for the button's content.
 * }}
 * 
 * @snippet children - The content to be displayed inside the button.
 * 
 * CSS Variables for Styling:
 * --mm-button-background-color: Button background
 * --mm-button-color: Button text color
 * --mm-button-border: Button border
 * --mm-button-border-radius: Button corner radius
 * --mm-button-background-color-hover: Hover state background
 * --mm-button-color-hover: Hover state text color
 * --mm-button-background-color-active: Active state background
 * --mm-button-color-active: Active state text color
 * 
 * For a complete list of CSS variables, see the Button component documentation.
 */
